title: scyphiphora malayana
intro: Scyphiphora malayana, also known as the 'Sea Pangolin', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Scyphiphora malayana include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
